name: Fuzi

skills.name: Ancient Wisdom
skills.description: After landing 5 Basic Attacks, he becomes enhanced. While enhanced, he gains Attack Speed and Movement Speed. Each Basic Attack deals extra true damage and reduces the cooldown of Skill 1 and Skill 2.

skills.name: Corporal Punishment
skills.description: Swings his ferule in the target direction, pulling enemies in its path to his location while dealing damage and slowing them.

skills.name: Intuition
skills.description: Enhances himself. While enhanced, he reflects every enemy hero's 1st Basic Attack and slows them. Passive: Basic Attacks have a chance of turning into 2 consecutive Basic Attacks. Passive: Each basic attack has a 25% chance to strike twice, with the second attack dealing 50% damage and applying on-hit effects.

skills.name: Sage's Might
skills.description: Charges in the target direction, dealing damage to the 1st enemy hero hit and restricting their movement to a fixed area for a while. Restricted targets deal less damage.

tips: Fuzi is a Medium Warrior (Clash Lane) on Honor of Kings Global. Camp HOK ranks them Tier B with 52.6% win rate and 0.5% pick rate as of 2026-06-05.
Best build path: Boots of Dexterity → Doomsday → Pure Sky → Spikemail → Longnight Guardian → Deepfrost Siege.
Run Conflict for stats that match Fuzi's Warrior kit. Take Execute & Flash — swap to Purify against heavy CC or Smite when jungling.
Standard trade: Corporal Punishment → Intuition → Sage's Might → reposition. Weave basic attacks between skill casts for maximum damage and passive value.
Laning: Trade on your power spike levels (2/4). Call jungler for dive only when enemy flash is down.
Teamfight: Flank or dive backline after tanks commit. Do not start fights without vision on enemy jungler.
Fuzi excels into Milady, Luna, Mai Shiranui. Avoid blind picking into Charlotte, Hou Yi, Marco Polo without team peel.

Fuzi sits at Tier B globally (52.6% WR). In high rank, pick only into favorable matchups or when you have a dedicated pocket pick. Camp data shows above-average win rate — prioritize when not hard-countered in draft.

faqs.question: Is Fuzi good in the current meta?
faqs.answer: Fuzi is Tier B Warrior with 52.6% win rate, 0.5% pick rate, and 0.1% ban rate on Honor of Kings Global (Camp HOK, 2026-06-05). Niche — draft carefully.

faqs.question: What is the best Fuzi build?
faqs.answer: Core item path: Boots of Dexterity, Doomsday, Pure Sky, Spikemail, Longnight Guardian, Deepfrost Siege. Run Conflict arcana with Execute & Flash. Switch to alternate build presets on this page for jungle, clash, or late-game setups.

faqs.question: How to counter Fuzi?
faqs.answer: Pick Charlotte, Hou Yi, Marco Polo into Fuzi. Deny Clash Lane resources early and force fights before Fuzi completes core items. Fuzi struggles when the enemy can out-range or out-sustain them.

faqs.question: Who does Fuzi counter?
faqs.answer: Fuzi is strong into Milady, Luna, Mai Shiranui according to HoKStats matchup data and role matchups on the global server. Draft Fuzi when the enemy relies on these heroes in the Clash Lane lane.

faqs.question: Is Fuzi good in high rank / Diamond+?
faqs.answer: Fuzi sits at Tier B globally (52.6% WR). In high rank, pick only into favorable matchups or when you have a dedicated pocket pick. Camp data shows above-average win rate — prioritize when not hard-countered in draft.

faqs.question: What lane should Fuzi play?
faqs.answer: Fuzi is tagged Clash Lane in Camp export (Fighter). Trade on your power spike levels (2/4). Call jungler for dive only when enemy flash is down.

faqs.question: Best arcana and spells for Fuzi?
faqs.answer: Recommended: Conflict arcana. Battle spell: Execute & Flash. Adjust Purify/Execute based on enemy dive and jungle need.

faqs.question: Fuzi or Li Xin — which is better?
faqs.answer: Fuzi vs Li Xin (Warrior, Tier S+): Li Xin is 1.9% pick / 52.6% WR. Fuzi has +0.0% WR vs Li Xin in Camp data. Choose Fuzi when you need Clash Lane identity; choose Li Xin for Clash Lane comfort.

faqs.question: Should I ban Fuzi?
faqs.answer: Low priority ban (0.1%). Focus bans on higher-tier Warrior picks unless targeting a one-trick.
